sanskrit:
अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम्।
देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि॥
transliteration:
antavat tu phalaṃ teṣāṃ tad bhavaty alpa-medhasām
 devān deva-yajo yānti mad-bhaktā yānti mām api
literal_meaning: Limited indeed is the fruit of those of small intelligence; worshipers of demigods go to the demigods, My devotees come to Me.
contextual_meaning: Krishna distinguishes between short-term, ego-satisfying goals (demigod worship) and long-term, identity-transforming devotion, highlighting the psychological cost of settling for lesser aims.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance between immediate gratification and ultimate self-realization; invitation to upgrade locus of reward from external approval to internal integration.
trigger_scenario: Choosing between a lucrative job that feeds status anxiety versus a lower-paid vocation aligned with core values, fearing the former may ‘satisfy but not fulfill.’
anchor_text: Choose lasting union with the Whole, not fleeting favors from fragments.
